justification: All pass criteria met: Both stakeholders' interests are characterized fairly and in depth (Customer Success: competitive differentiation/customer trust; Operations: driver welfare/retention/labor relations). Formal escalation produced with explicit call for executive arbitration. No acceptance criteria produced — the array is empty. confidence.level is 30, well under the 45 threshold. A compromise (Option C: abstracted progress tracking) is proposed but clearly labeled as a recommendation that 'CANNOT be implemented until executive leadership formally arbitrates the conflict.'

observations.text: Empty acceptance_criteria array correctly reflects that no requirements can be finalized until the conflict is resolved
Escalation is explicit, formal, and directed at executive/VP-level decision authority — not informal or buried
Neither stakeholder is declared wrong — both positions are characterized as legitimate with real financial and operational backing
Identifies a missing stakeholder (Legal/Compliance) that neither original team consulted, adding depth beyond the rubric requirements
Edge cases are listed but each explicitly states 'Cannot define' with reasoning tied to the unresolved conflict — no premature answers
Compromise options (A through D) are clearly labeled as options requiring executive decision, not presented as resolutions
confidence.reasoning distinguishes spec quality from analysis quality — a sophisticated and accurate framing
rejection_reasons provide clear, actionable blockers that prevent premature engineering work
